title: Politics Truth Stack
summary: Politics is the study of power, institutions, public decision-making, legitimacy, and how societies organise collective life.
starterPack: Start with Parliament, the AEC, and ABS.
Read Lowy Institute and Chatham House for interpretation.

tiers.title: First Principles
tiers.description: Politics is structured conflict, legitimacy, and coordination under scarcity.

items.name: Power is distributed
items.description: Institutions, incentives, and information shape outcomes.

items.name: Legitimacy matters
items.description: Rules only work when people accept them as valid.

items.name: Every decision has tradeoffs
items.description: Public choices create winners, losers, and second-order effects.

tiers.title: Primary Authorities
tiers.description: The official sources that anchor political reality.

tiers.title: Mature Interpreters
tiers.description: Analysts who explain politics with institutional seriousness.

items.name: Chatham House
items.description: Policy analysis with strong institutional credibility.

items.name: Lowy Institute
items.description: Australian lens on geopolitics and policy.

tiers.title: Practitioners
tiers.description: The people who make politics operate every day.

items.name: Parliamentary staff
items.description: Committee, briefing, and legislative support work.

items.name: Campaign managers
items.description: Messaging, field work, and election operations.

tiers.title: Institutions
tiers.description: The structures where political power is formalised.

tiers.title: Media and Publications
tiers.description: Public writing that helps separate signal from noise.

items.name: Inside Story
items.description: Deep Australian policy and politics coverage.
